name: UK Biobank
description: One of the world's largest prospective cohort studies with deep phenotyping of 500,000+ participants aged 40-69 at recruitment. Includes genomics, imaging, biomarkers, and newly linked GP data. All access now via the cloud-based Research Analysis Platform (RAP).
key_datasets: Primary care GP data (newly linked Feb 2026)
tre_platform: UK Biobank Research Analysis Platform (RAP) on DNAnexus

requirements.name: Researcher registration
requirements.description: Register as a bona fide researcher on the UK Biobank Access Management System

requirements.name: Research application
requirements.description: Submit a research application describing the project, data needed, and public benefit

requirements.name: Material Transfer Agreement
requirements.description: Institution must sign the MTA governing data use terms

access_steps.name: Register as researcher
access_steps.description: Create account on Access Management System and verify credentials

access_steps.name: Submit research application
access_steps.description: Describe research question, data fields needed, analysis plan

access_steps.name: Access Committee review
access_steps.description: Application reviewed for scientific merit and alignment with participant consent

access_steps.name: MTA signed
access_steps.description: Institutional Material Transfer Agreement negotiated and signed

access_steps.name: RAP access provisioned
access_steps.description: Workspace created on the Research Analysis Platform with approved datasets

costs.notes: Fee varies by dataset scope. Industry rates higher. RAP cloud compute charged separately.

strengths: Newly linked GP primary care data (Feb 2026)
limitations: Volunteer cohort - not population-representative (healthy volunteer bias)
RAP cloud compute adds ongoing costs
recent_changes: GP data linkage enabled (government decision Feb 2026)
All access now via Research Analysis Platform (RAP) - no more data downloads
